naksh_shetty.name: Naksh Shetty
naksh_shetty.summary:
Hailing from California, Naksh Shetty is a U.S. Junior National Champion — crowned at the biggest event on the domestic calendar in BS U11. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 15 career victories across 13 tournaments, Naksh has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Notably, Naksh has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Naksh is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 6 singles titles, 4 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (BS U11) • 9 ORC titles.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Naksh boasts 12 three-game battles — proving Naksh is no stranger to the pressure of a decider, 56 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, an extraordinary 83% match win rate across 76 matches.

Naksh has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 9 different players:
  • Xiner Hu — 8 events in XD, producing 3 titles together
  • Iniyan Arun — 3 events in BD, producing 2 titles together
  • Fonghong Lin — 3 events in BD, best: #2

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 4 tournaments. Naksh is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Naksh has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Naksh has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 7 seeded entries across the career, Naksh consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 15 titles, Naksh is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Hailing from North Carolina, Nithesh Haribabu has reached the podium at Junior Nationals, finishing top-3 in BS U17. At the biggest event on the calendar, making the final four is a statement: Nithesh belongs with the best in the country.

Nithesh is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Nithesh competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 3 singles titles, a doubles best of #2, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (1 entry) • Junior Nationals top-3 (BS U17) • 1 ORC title.

Nithesh has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 16 different players:
  • Mark w Wu — 2 events in BD, best: #3

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 2 titles across 3 tournaments — a clear sign that Nithesh is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Nithesh has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Nithesh has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 12 seeded entries across the career, Nithesh consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 6 seasons and 4 titles, Nithesh has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.
